findings: The change removes several npm dependencies and deletes the lockfile, which can affect install resolution and runtime behavior; there are no tests in the diff to verify the app still builds and runs with the reduced dependency set.

Test Suggestion 1: Run a clean install followed by a production build to verify the package manifest and removed lockfile still resolve correctly across environments, and that no missing dependency breaks compilation.

Test Suggestion 2: Add an integration test that renders or imports any UI paths that previously depended on the removed packages (cmdk, embla-carousel-react, input-otp, vaul) to confirm there are no hidden runtime imports or dead code paths still referencing them.

Test Suggestion 3: Add a dependency-safety check that exercises the app startup and a representative page flow after reinstalling from scratch, to catch failures caused by transitive dependency changes or peer dependency mismatches introduced by deleting pnpm-lock.yaml.

No security issues detected. The diff only removes unused npm dependencies from package.json and deletes pnpm-lock.yaml; there are no changes to authentication/authorization logic, secret handling, or input handling sinks.
Primary driver: dependency manifest cleanup only; no executable branching logic changed

P1: Removing multiple npm dependencies and deleting pnpm-lock.yaml can change install resolution and runtime behavior; add a clean install + production build check to verify the app still compiles and runs with the reduced dependency set [TestQuality] - Validate from a fresh install before merging.

P1: There are no tests covering paths that may still reference the removed packages (cmdk, embla-carousel-react, input-otp, vaul); add an integration test or import/build check to catch hidden runtime imports [TestQuality] - Confirm no dead code or indirect imports remain.
